rawText:
There is a widespread misconception that simple explanations are for beginners and that complexity is a sign of depth, but the opposite is more often true: the ability to explain a difficult idea in plain language is one of the clearest indicators that the explainer genuinely understands it.

Richard Feynman, the Nobel Prize–winning physicist, famously tested his own understanding by trying to explain any concept as if he were teaching it to a first-year student — and when he could not, he treated the failure not as a limitation of the audience but as a gap in his own knowledge.

This approach works because simplifying forces the explainer to identify the core mechanism — the one thing that, if understood, makes everything else fall into place — and to strip away the jargon, qualifications, and tangential details that obscure it.

Jargon is particularly insidious because it gives the illusion of communication while actually functioning as a gate: people inside the field understand the shorthand, but everyone outside is excluded without being told explicitly that they are excluded.

A doctor who tells a patient "you have idiopathic thrombocytopenia" has communicated a diagnosis but not an understanding; a doctor who says "your blood is not clotting properly, and we do not yet know why" has given the patient something far more useful — a mental model of the problem and an honest acknowledgment of uncertainty.

The skill of simplification is not about dumbing things down; it is about finding the right level of abstraction for the audience in front of you.

An explanation that works for a ten-year-old will bore an expert, and an explanation pitched at a graduate seminar will alienate a general audience — the art lies in reading the room and adjusting accordingly.

Analogies are among the most powerful tools for this purpose, because they map unfamiliar concepts onto structures the listener already understands.

Saying "DNA is like a recipe book for building a body" is not scientifically precise, but it gives a non-specialist a functional framework that more precise language would not.

The best explainers also know when to stop — they resist the temptation to add "just one more detail" and instead leave the listener with a clean, memorable takeaway that can serve as a foundation for deeper learning later.

Overexplaining is a surprisingly common failure mode, driven by the explainer's anxiety that the audience might miss something, but the effect is usually the opposite: too much information overwhelms the listener and obscures the very point it was meant to clarify.

In a world that grows more specialized every year, the people who can translate between domains — who can take what the scientist discovered, what the engineer built, or what the policy analyst recommended and make it legible to a broader public — perform a service that is as valuable as the original work itself.

Explaining hard things simply is not a lesser form of intelligence; it is intelligence in its most generous and socially useful form.

If you can make someone else understand what you understand, you have done more than demonstrate knowledge — you have created it in another mind, and that is the deepest purpose of communication.

overview.title: The Skill of Explaining Hard Things Simply

sentences.text: Some people think using hard words makes you sound smart, but actually, explaining things simply is harder and shows real understanding.
sentences.translation: 어려운 단어를 쓰면 똑똑해 보인다고 생각하는 사람들이 있지만, 사실 쉽게 설명하는 게 더 어렵고 진짜 이해를 보여줘요.

sentences.text: A famous scientist named Feynman always tried to explain ideas so simply that even a new student could understand them.
sentences.translation: 파인만이라는 유명한 과학자는 항상 새 학생도 이해할 수 있을 만큼 쉽게 설명하려고 노력했어요.

sentences.text: Special words that only experts know can make other people feel left out, even if the speaker does not mean to.
sentences.translation: 전문가만 아는 특별한 단어들은 말하는 사람이 의도하지 않더라도 다른 사람들을 소외시킬 수 있어요.

sentences.text: Comparing something new to something familiar — like saying DNA is a recipe book — helps people understand faster.
sentences.translation: DNA가 요리책이라고 말하는 것처럼, 새로운 것을 친숙한 것에 비유하면 사람들이 더 빨리 이해해요.

sentences.text: Saying too much can actually confuse people more than saying just enough.
sentences.translation: 너무 많이 말하면 적당히 말하는 것보다 오히려 사람들을 더 혼란스럽게 할 수 있어요.

sentences.text: Helping someone else understand something you know is one of the most important things you can do.
sentences.translation: 내가 아는 것을 다른 사람이 이해하도록 돕는 것은 할 수 있는 가장 중요한 일 중 하나예요.

sentences.text: The essay argues that explaining complex ideas simply is not a sign of shallow thinking but proof of deep understanding.
sentences.translation: 이 에세이는 복잡한 아이디어를 쉽게 설명하는 것이 얕은 사고의 표시가 아니라 깊은 이해의 증거라고 주장해요.

sentences.text: Richard Feynman used simplification as a diagnostic tool: if he could not explain something simply, he knew he did not fully understand it.
sentences.translation: 리처드 파인만은 단순화를 진단 도구로 사용했어요: 무언가를 쉽게 설명할 수 없으면, 자신이 완전히 이해하지 못한 것이라고 여겼어요.

sentences.text: Jargon creates an invisible barrier that excludes non-experts from conversations they have a right to join.
sentences.translation: 전문 용어는 비전문가를 참여할 권리가 있는 대화에서 배제하는 보이지 않는 장벽을 만들어요.

sentences.text: Effective explainers use analogies to map unfamiliar concepts onto structures the audience already knows.
sentences.translation: 효과적인 설명자는 비유를 사용해 낯선 개념을 청중이 이미 아는 구조에 대응시켜요.

sentences.text: Overexplaining is a common failure driven by anxiety, and it often obscures the very point the speaker intended to clarify.
sentences.translation: 과도한 설명은 불안에 의해 생기는 흔한 실패이며, 화자가 명확히 하려던 바로 그 요점을 흐리게 만들어요.

sentences.text: The ability to translate expert knowledge for a broader public is described as intelligence in its most generous and socially useful form.
sentences.translation: 전문 지식을 더 넓은 대중에게 번역하는 능력은 가장 관대하고 사회적으로 유용한 형태의 지성으로 묘사돼요.

sentences.text: This meta-cognitive essay inverts a common intellectual hierarchy by arguing that the capacity for simplification supersedes the capacity for complexity as evidence of mastery.
sentences.translation: 이 메타인지적 에세이는 단순화 능력이 숙달의 증거로서 복잡성 능력을 능가한다고 주장함으로써 흔한 지적 위계를 뒤집어요.

sentences.text: The writer deploys Feynman as an authoritative exemplar, then builds a structural argument moving from individual cognition to social gatekeeping to cross-domain translation.
sentences.translation: 글쓴이는 파인만을 권위 있는 범례로 활용한 뒤, 개인 인지에서 사회적 문지기 역할, 영역 간 번역으로 이어지는 구조적 논증을 구축해요.

sentences.text: The medical vignette — idiopathic thrombocytopenia versus plain-language paraphrase — functions as a concrete proof-of-concept for the essay's central claim.
sentences.translation: 의학적 삽화 — 특발성 혈소판감소증 대 일상어 환언 — 는 에세이의 핵심 주장에 대한 구체적 개념 증명으로 기능해요.

sentences.text: Rhetorical devices include contrastive semicolons, appositive dashes, and a closing sentence that reframes communication as an act of epistemic creation.
sentences.translation: 수사적 장치로는 대조적 세미콜론, 동격 대시, 그리고 의사소통을 인식론적 창조 행위로 재구성하는 결론 문장이 포함돼요.

sentences.text: The essay employs hedging ("more often true," "in many cases") alongside categorical assertions ("the deepest purpose of communication"), balancing academic caution with persuasive conviction.
sentences.translation: 이 에세이는 완화 표현("더 자주 사실인", "많은 경우")을 단정적 주장("소통의 가장 깊은 목적")과 함께 사용해 학술적 신중함과 설득적 확신의 균형을 맞춰요.

sentences.text: The concluding argument elevates explanation from knowledge transfer to knowledge creation, positioning clear communication as generative rather than merely reproductive.
sentences.translation: 결론적 논증은 설명을 지식 전달에서 지식 창조로 격상시키며, 명확한 소통을 단순히 재생산적인 것이 아니라 생성적인 것으로 자리매김해요.

sentences.original: There is a widespread misconception that simple explanations are for beginners and that complexity is a sign of depth, but the opposite is more often true: the ability to explain a difficult idea in plain language is one of the clearest indicators that the explainer genuinely understands it.
sentences.translation: 간단한 설명은 초보자용이고 복잡성이 깊이의 표시라는 널리 퍼진 오해가 있지만, 그 반대가 더 자주 사실이다: 어려운 아이디어를 평이한 언어로 설명하는 능력이야말로 설명하는 사람이 진정으로 이해하고 있다는 가장 분명한 지표 중 하나이다.

grammarElements.text: There
grammarElements.label: 주어 (유도부사 구문)

grammarElements.text: is
grammarElements.label: 동사 (현재형)

grammarElements.text: a widespread misconception that simple explanations are for beginners and that complexity is a sign of depth
grammarElements.label: 보어 (동격 that절 2개 포함 명사구)

grammarElements.text: but
grammarElements.label: 등위접속사 (반전)

grammarElements.text: the opposite is more often true
grammarElements.label: 반전절

grammarElements.text: the ability to explain a difficult idea in plain language is one of the clearest indicators that the explainer genuinely understands it
grammarElements.label: 콜론 뒤 설명절 (that 관계절 포함)

vocabulary.example: There is a widespread belief that more is always better.
It is a common misconception that vaccines cause illness.
Test scores are not always reliable indicators of ability.

expressions.phrase: the opposite is more often true
expressions.usage: 흔한 믿음을 반박하며 논점을 전환할 때 쓰는 표현
전문 용어 없이 누구나 이해할 수 있는 말로 표현한다는 뜻

patterns.pattern: There is a widespread misconception that [A] and that [B], but the opposite is more often true: [corrected claim]
patterns.explanation: 널리 퍼진 오해를 먼저 제시하고 but으로 반전시킨 뒤, 콜론 뒤에 올바른 주장을 펼치는 도입 패턴. 에세이 전체의 논지를 첫 문장에서 선언한다.
examples: There is a widespread misconception that speed equals efficiency and that busy people are productive, but the opposite is more often true: the most effective workers protect their focus by doing fewer things better.

There is a widespread misconception that confidence comes before action and that readiness precedes performance, but the opposite is more often true: confidence grows from doing the thing you were afraid to try.

drillQuestions.question: There is a widespread _____ that simple explanations are for beginners.
The ability to explain a difficult idea in _____ language is an indicator of understanding.
"The opposite is more often _____" reverses the initial claim.
"Widespread" means something that is _____ across many people.

sentences.notes: 글의 도입부로, "There is a widespread misconception" 유도부사 구문으로 통념을 먼저 세운 뒤 but으로 전복시킨다. 콜론 뒤의 긴 설명절이 에세이 전체의 핵심 논지를 선언한다. 이중 that절(that simple ~ and that complexity ~)이 통념의 두 축을 병렬로 제시하는 고급 구조이다.

koreanAlignment.en: There is a widespread misconception
that simple explanations are for beginners
and that complexity is a sign of depth
but the opposite is more often true
the ability to explain a difficult idea in plain language
koreanAlignment.ko: 어려운 아이디어를 평이한 언어로 설명하는 능력이야말로

koreanAlignment.en: is one of the clearest indicators that the explainer genuinely understands it
koreanAlignment.ko: 설명하는 사람이 진정으로 이해하고 있다는 가장 분명한 지표 중 하나이다

pronunciationNotes: misconception: /ˌmɪskənˈsepʃən/ — 세 번째 음절에 강세. "미스컨셉션"에 가깝다. mis- 접두사가 "잘못된"을 뜻한다.
genuinely: /ˈdʒenjuɪnli/ — 첫 음절에 강세. "제뉴인리"에 가깝다. -ly가 붙어도 강세 위치 변하지 않음.

sentences.original: Richard Feynman, the Nobel Prize–winning physicist, famously tested his own understanding by trying to explain any concept as if he were teaching it to a first-year student — and when he could not, he treated the failure not as a limitation of the audience but as a gap in his own knowledge.
sentences.translation: 노벨상을 수상한 물리학자 리처드 파인만은 어떤 개념이든 1학년 학생에게 가르치듯 설명해 봄으로써 자신의 이해도를 테스트한 것으로 유명한데 — 그렇게 할 수 없을 때, 그는 그 실패를 청중의 한계가 아니라 자기 자신의 지식의 빈틈으로 여겼다.

grammarElements.text: Richard Feynman, the Nobel Prize–winning physicist,
grammarElements.label: 주어 (고유명사 + 동격 삽입구)

grammarElements.text: famously tested
grammarElements.label: 부사 + 동사 (과거형)

grammarElements.text: his own understanding
grammarElements.label: 목적어

grammarElements.text: by trying to explain any concept as if he were teaching it to a first-year student
grammarElements.label: 전치사구 (by + 동명사 + as if 가정법)

grammarElements.text: and when he could not, he treated the failure not as a limitation of the audience but as a gap in his own knowledge
grammarElements.label: 병렬절 (when 시간절 + not A but B)

vocabulary.example: Einstein famously failed his first college entrance exam.
The failure was not a limitation of the student.
There is a gap between what we know and what we think we know.

expressions.usage: as if + 가정법 과거로 가상의 상황을 설정하는 격식적 표현

expressions.phrase: not as a limitation of ... but as a gap in
expressions.usage: not A but B 구조로 책임 귀속을 전환하는 표현

patterns.pattern: [Authority figure] famously [tested/demonstrated] [concept] by [method] — and when [failure], [he/she] treated it not as [blame on others] but as [self-reflection]
patterns.explanation: 권위 있는 인물의 방법론을 소개하고, 대시 뒤에 실패 시의 자기 성찰적 태도를 not A but B로 제시하는 패턴. 학술 에세이에서 권위 있는 사례를 도입하는 전형적 구조이다.
examples: Marie Curie famously tested her hypotheses by repeating experiments under extreme conditions — and when results contradicted her expectations, she treated the discrepancy not as a flaw in the method but as a clue to something unknown.
Darwin famously tested his theory by seeking counterexamples — and when he found them, he treated them not as threats to his argument but as opportunities to refine it.

drillQuestions.question: Feynman tested his understanding by trying to _____ any concept simply.
He treated failure not as a limitation of the _____ but as his own gap.
"As if he were teaching" uses the _____ mood.
"A gap in his own knowledge" means a _____ in understanding.

sentences.notes: 파인만이라는 권위 있는 인물을 동격 삽입구(the Nobel Prize–winning physicist)로 소개하고, as if 가정법(as if he were teaching)으로 그의 방법론을 묘사한다. "not as A but as B" 구조가 실패의 해석을 전환하며, 전체 에세이의 핵심 태도 — 설명 실패는 청중이 아닌 설명자의 문제 — 를 확립한다.

koreanAlignment.en: Richard Feynman, the Nobel Prize–winning physicist,
famously tested his own understanding
by trying to explain any concept
as if he were teaching it to a first-year student
and when he could not
he treated the failure not as a limitation of the audience but as a gap in his own knowledge
koreanAlignment.ko: 그는 그 실패를 청중의 한계가 아니라 자기 자신의 지식의 빈틈으로 여겼다

pronunciationNotes: Feynman: /ˈfaɪnmən/ — 첫 음절에 강세. "파인먼"에 가깝다. ey를 /aɪ/로 발음.
physicist: /ˈfɪzɪsɪst/ — 첫 음절에 강세. "피지시스트"에 가깝다. ph를 /f/로 발음.
sentences.rhetoricalDevice: 권위 있는 사례 도입 (argument from authority)

sentences.original: This approach works because simplifying forces the explainer to identify the core mechanism — the one thing that, if understood, makes everything else fall into place — and to strip away the jargon, qualifications, and tangential details that obscure it.
sentences.translation: 이 접근법이 효과적인 이유는 단순화가 설명자로 하여금 핵심 메커니즘 — 이해되면 다른 모든 것이 제자리에 놓이게 만드는 그 하나 — 을 파악하고, 그것을 흐리게 하는 전문 용어, 단서 조건, 그리고 지엽적 세부 사항을 벗겨 내도록 강제하기 때문이다.

grammarElements.text: This approach
grammarElements.label: 주어 (지시 형용사 + 명사)

grammarElements.text: works
grammarElements.label: 동사 (현재형)

grammarElements.text: because simplifying forces the explainer to identify the core mechanism
grammarElements.label: because 이유절 (force + to 부정사)

grammarElements.text: the one thing that, if understood, makes everything else fall into place
grammarElements.label: 대시 삽입 동격구 (조건 삽입절 포함 관계절)

grammarElements.text: and to strip away the jargon, qualifications, and tangential details that obscure it
grammarElements.label: 병렬 to 부정사 + 관계절

vocabulary.example: Understanding the core mechanism makes the rest easier.
The report was full of legal jargon.
The speaker kept making tangential points that distracted from the main argument.
Too many details can obscure the main point.

expressions.usage: 여러 조각이 맞아떨어져 전체가 이해되는 순간을 표현하는 관용구
불필요한 것을 걷어내어 본질을 드러낸다는 뜻

patterns.pattern: This approach works because [process] forces [person] to identify [core element] — [appositive] — and to strip away [distractions] that obscure it
patterns.explanation: 접근법의 효과를 because절로 설명하고, 대시 삽입 동격구로 핵심 요소를 부연한 뒤, 병렬 to 부정사로 제거해야 할 것을 나열하는 패턴.
examples: This method works because editing forces the writer to identify the central argument — the one claim that everything else supports — and to strip away the repetition, hedging, and side points that dilute it.

This technique works because budgeting forces the planner to identify the essential expenses — the ones that, if cut, would cause real harm — and to strip away the luxuries, subscriptions, and impulse purchases that inflate the total.

drillQuestions.question: Simplifying forces the explainer to identify the core _____.
"Fall into place" means everything starts to make _____.
"Strip away" means to _____ unnecessary things.
"Tangential details" are details that are _____ to the main point.

sentences.notes: 대시 삽입 동격구 안에 조건 삽입절(if understood)이 포함된 삼중 내포 구조가 특징이다. "forces the explainer to identify ~ and to strip away ~"의 병렬 to 부정사가 단순화 과정의 두 측면 — 핵심 파악과 불필요한 제거 — 을 대비시킨다.

koreanAlignment.en: This approach works because
simplifying forces the explainer to identify the core mechanism
koreanAlignment.ko: 단순화가 설명자로 하여금 핵심 메커니즘을 파악하도록 강제하기 때문이다

koreanAlignment.en: the one thing that, if understood, makes everything else fall into place
koreanAlignment.ko: 이해되면 다른 모든 것이 제자리에 놓이게 만드는 그 하나

koreanAlignment.en: and to strip away the jargon, qualifications, and tangential details
koreanAlignment.ko: 그리고 전문 용어, 단서 조건, 지엽적 세부 사항을 벗겨 내도록

koreanAlignment.en: that obscure it

pronunciationNotes: mechanism: /ˈmekənɪzəm/ — 첫 음절에 강세. "메커니즘"에 가깝다. ch를 /k/로 발음.
tangential: /tænˈdʒenʃəl/ — 두 번째 음절에 강세. "탠젠셜"에 가깝다. g를 /dʒ/로 발음.

sentences.original: Jargon is particularly insidious because it gives the illusion of communication while actually functioning as a gate: people inside the field understand the shorthand, but everyone outside is excluded without being told explicitly that they are excluded.
sentences.translation: 전문 용어는 특히 교활한데, 실제로는 문지기 역할을 하면서도 소통의 환상을 주기 때문이다: 분야 내부의 사람들은 그 약칭을 이해하지만, 외부의 모든 사람은 자신이 배제되었다는 것을 명시적으로 듣지 못한 채 배제된다.

grammarElements.text: Jargon
grammarElements.label: 주어 (추상 명사)

grammarElements.text: is
grammarElements.label: 동사 (현재형)

grammarElements.text: particularly insidious
grammarElements.label: 보어 (부사 + 형용사)

grammarElements.text: because it gives the illusion of communication while actually functioning as a gate
grammarElements.label: because 이유절 (while 동시절 포함)

grammarElements.text: people inside the field understand the shorthand, but everyone outside is excluded without being told explicitly that they are excluded
grammarElements.label: 콜론 뒤 설명절 (but 대조)

vocabulary.example: The most insidious problems are those you do not notice until it is too late.
The jargon creates an illusion of understanding.
Technical shorthand saves time for experts but confuses outsiders.
No one told them explicitly that they were not welcome.

expressions.usage: 겉으로는 소통하는 것 같지만 실제로는 그렇지 않다는 비판적 표현
특정 집단만 통과시키고 나머지는 차단하는 기능을 비유적으로 표현

patterns.pattern: [Subject] is particularly insidious because it gives the illusion of [positive] while actually functioning as [negative]: [insiders] understand, but [outsiders] are excluded
patterns.explanation: 어떤 것이 겉으로는 긍정적이지만 실제로는 배타적으로 기능한다는 역설을 콜론 뒤 내부자/외부자 대비로 풀어내는 패턴.
examples: Politeness is particularly insidious when it gives the illusion of agreement while actually functioning as dismissal: the speaker feels heard, but the listener has already decided to ignore the suggestion.

Meritocracy is particularly insidious because it gives the illusion of fairness while actually functioning as a filter: those with advantages succeed, but those without are blamed for their own exclusion.

drillQuestions.question: Jargon is particularly _____ because it hides exclusion behind apparent communication.
Jargon gives the _____ of communication.
People outside the field are _____ without being told.
"Shorthand" is a quick way of _____ something within a specialized group.

sentences.notes: "insidious"라는 강한 형용사가 전문 용어의 해로움에 대한 글쓴이의 비판적 입장을 드러낸다. while 동시절(while actually functioning as a gate)이 겉과 속의 괴리를 포착하고, 콜론 뒤 대조(people inside ~ but everyone outside ~)가 구체적 불평등 구조를 밝힌다.

koreanAlignment.en: Jargon is particularly insidious
because it gives the illusion of communication
while actually functioning as a gate
people inside the field understand the shorthand
koreanAlignment.ko: 분야 내부의 사람들은 그 약칭을 이해하지만

koreanAlignment.en: but everyone outside is excluded without being told explicitly that they are excluded
koreanAlignment.ko: 외부의 모든 사람은 명시적으로 듣지 못한 채 배제된다

pronunciationNotes: insidious: /ɪnˈsɪdiəs/ — 두 번째 음절에 강세. "인시디어스"에 가깝다. -ious를 /iəs/로 발음.
explicitly: /ɪkˈsplɪsɪtli/ — 두 번째 음절에 강세. "익스플리시틀리"에 가깝다. x를 /ks/로 발음.
sentences.rhetoricalDevice: 은폐된 배제 비판 (critique of hidden exclusion)

sentences.original: A doctor who tells a patient "you have idiopathic thrombocytopenia" has communicated a diagnosis but not an understanding; a doctor who says "your blood is not clotting properly, and we do not yet know why" has given the patient something far more useful — a mental model of the problem and an honest acknowledgment of uncertainty.
sentences.translation: 환자에게 "특발성 혈소판감소증입니다"라고 말하는 의사는 진단을 전달했지만 이해를 전달하지는 못했다; "혈액이 제대로 응고되지 않고 있으며, 아직 이유를 모릅니다"라고 말하는 의사는 환자에게 훨씬 더 유용한 것을 준 것이다 — 문제에 대한 정신적 모형과 불확실성에 대한 솔직한 인정을.

grammarElements.text: A doctor who tells a patient "you have idiopathic thrombocytopenia"
grammarElements.label: 주어 (관계절 + 직접 인용문 포함)

grammarElements.text: has communicated
grammarElements.label: 동사 (현재완료)

grammarElements.text: a diagnosis but not an understanding
grammarElements.label: 목적어 (부분 부정)

grammarElements.text: a doctor who says "your blood is not clotting properly, and we do not yet know why"
grammarElements.label: 세미콜론 뒤 주어 (관계절 + 직접 인용문)

grammarElements.text: has given the patient something far more useful — a mental model of the problem and an honest acknowledgment of uncertainty
grammarElements.label: 동사 + 간접목적어 + 직접목적어 + 대시 동격구

vocabulary.example: Idiopathic means the cause is unknown.
A diagnosis without explanation is incomplete.
An honest acknowledgment of uncertainty builds trust.
Admitting uncertainty is a sign of intellectual honesty.

expressions.phrase: a mental model of the problem
expressions.usage: 복잡한 문제를 이해하기 위한 단순화된 내적 표상을 가리키는 학술적 표현

expressions.phrase: communicated a diagnosis but not an understanding
expressions.usage: 정보 전달과 의미 전달의 차이를 부각하는 대조 표현

patterns.pattern: [Person] who [uses jargon] has communicated [label] but not [understanding]; [Person] who [uses plain language] has given [audience] something far more useful — [what it actually is]
patterns.explanation: 세미콜론으로 두 시나리오를 대비시키고, 대시 뒤에 평이한 설명이 제공하는 진짜 가치를 구체화하는 패턴. 의학적 삽화가 추상적 논점을 생생하게 증명한다.
examples: A lawyer who says "the defendant faces tortious liability" has communicated a legal status but not a meaning; a lawyer who says "they could be held responsible for the harm they caused" has given the client something far more useful — a clear picture of the risk.

A teacher who says "this demonstrates the Dunning-Kruger effect" has communicated a label but not a lesson; a teacher who says "the less you know, the more confident you tend to feel" has given the student something far more useful — a tool for self-awareness.

drillQuestions.question: The doctor who used jargon communicated a _____ but not an understanding.
"Idiopathic" means the cause is _____.
The plain-language doctor gave the patient a mental _____ of the problem.
"An honest acknowledgment of uncertainty" means admitting you do not _____.

sentences.notes: 이 글에서 가장 긴 문장 중 하나로, 세미콜론이 두 개의 대조적 시나리오를 연결한다. 전문 용어(idiopathic thrombocytopenia)와 평이한 설명(your blood is not clotting properly)의 직접 비교가 에세이의 핵심 논지를 구체적으로 증명한다. 대시 뒤의 동격구가 "이해"의 구성 요소를 명시한다.

koreanAlignment.en: A doctor who tells a patient "you have idiopathic thrombocytopenia"
has communicated a diagnosis but not an understanding
a doctor who says "your blood is not clotting properly, and we do not yet know why"
koreanAlignment.ko: "혈액이 제대로 응고되지 않고 있으며, 아직 이유를 모릅니다"라고 말하는 의사는

koreanAlignment.en: has given the patient something far more useful
koreanAlignment.ko: 환자에게 훨씬 더 유용한 것을 준 것이다

koreanAlignment.en: a mental model of the problem and an honest acknowledgment of uncertainty
koreanAlignment.ko: 문제에 대한 정신적 모형과 불확실성에 대한 솔직한 인정을

pronunciationNotes: idiopathic: /ˌɪdiəˈpæθɪk/ — 세 번째 음절에 강세. "이디어패딕"에 가깝다. th를 /θ/로 발음.
thrombocytopenia: /ˌθrɑːmboʊˌsaɪtəˈpiːniə/ — 다섯 번째 음절에 강세. "쓰롬보사이토피니아"에 가깝다. 의학 전문 용어.

sentences.original: The skill of simplification is not about dumbing things down; it is about finding the right level of abstraction for the audience in front of you.
sentences.translation: 단순화의 기술은 내용을 저급하게 만드는 것이 아니다; 눈앞의 청중에 맞는 적절한 추상화 수준을 찾는 것이다.

grammarElements.text: The skill of simplification
grammarElements.label: 주어 (of 전치사구 수식)

grammarElements.text: is not about
grammarElements.label: 동사 + 부정 + 전치사

grammarElements.text: dumbing things down
grammarElements.label: 보어 (동명사구)

grammarElements.text: it is about finding the right level of abstraction for the audience in front of you
grammarElements.label: 세미콜론 뒤 재정의절

vocabulary.example: Simplification requires deeper understanding than complication does.
Good teaching simplifies without dumbing things down.
Choosing the right level of abstraction is key to good explanation.

expressions.phrase: is not about ... it is about
expressions.usage: 세미콜론으로 연결된 not A; it is B 구조로 개념을 재정의하는 강력한 수사적 표현

expressions.phrase: the audience in front of you
expressions.usage: 추상적 "청중" 개념을 구체적이고 즉각적인 것으로 만드는 표현

patterns.pattern: The skill of [X] is not about [common misunderstanding]; it is about [correct definition]
patterns.explanation: 기술이나 능력에 대한 흔한 오해를 먼저 부정하고, 세미콜론 뒤에 올바른 정의를 제시하는 재정의 패턴. 간결한 문장에 핵심 논점이 압축된다.
examples: The skill of listening is not about staying silent; it is about understanding what the speaker needs you to hear.
The skill of leadership is not about giving orders; it is about creating conditions where others do their best work.

drillQuestions.question: Simplification is not about _____ things down.
It is about finding the right level of _____.
"The audience in front of you" emphasizes that explanation should be _____ to the specific listeners.
"Is not about ... it is about" is used to _____ a concept.

sentences.notes: 세미콜론으로 연결된 "is not about A; it is about B" 구조가 단순화의 본질을 재정의한다. "dumbing things down"이라는 구어적 표현과 "abstraction"이라는 학술적 표현의 대비가 이 문장 자체에서 적절한 언어 수준 조절을 실연한다.

koreanAlignment.en: The skill of simplification
is not about dumbing things down
it is about finding the right level of abstraction
for the audience in front of you

pronunciationNotes: simplification: /ˌsɪmplɪfɪˈkeɪʃən/ — 네 번째 음절에 강세. "심플리피케이션"에 가깝다.
abstraction: /æbˈstrækʃən/ — 두 번째 음절에 강세. "앱스트랙션"에 가깝다. bstr를 빠르게 연결.

sentences.original: An explanation that works for a ten-year-old will bore an expert, and an explanation pitched at a graduate seminar will alienate a general audience — the art lies in reading the room and adjusting accordingly.
sentences.translation: 열 살짜리에게 통하는 설명은 전문가를 지루하게 할 것이고, 대학원 세미나 수준으로 맞춰진 설명은 일반 청중을 소외시킬 것이다 — 기술은 분위기를 읽고 그에 따라 조정하는 데 있다.

grammarElements.text: An explanation that works for a ten-year-old
grammarElements.label: 주어 (관계절 수식)

grammarElements.text: will bore
grammarElements.label: 동사 (미래형)

grammarElements.text: an expert
grammarElements.label: 목적어

grammarElements.text: and an explanation pitched at a graduate seminar will alienate a general audience
grammarElements.label: 병렬절 (과거분사 수식 주어)

grammarElements.text: the art lies in reading the room and adjusting accordingly
grammarElements.label: 대시 뒤 결론절 (동명사 병렬)

vocabulary.example: The talk was pitched at a highly technical audience.
Using too much jargon will alienate your audience.
Read the room and adjust accordingly.

expressions.usage: 청중의 반응과 수준을 민감하게 감지한다는 뜻의 관용적 표현
어떤 일의 핵심 기술이 무엇인지 명시할 때 쓰는 격식적 표현

patterns.pattern: [Explanation for A] will [negative effect on B], and [explanation for C] will [negative effect on D] — the art lies in [balancing skill]
patterns.explanation: 양극단의 실패를 먼저 제시하고, 대시 뒤에 그 사이의 균형 잡기가 진짜 기술이라는 결론을 내리는 패턴.
examples: A plan that satisfies cautious investors will frustrate ambitious founders, and a plan designed for bold growth will alarm risk-averse stakeholders — the art lies in finding a middle path that addresses both.
A schedule that works for early risers will exhaust night owls, and a schedule built for night workers will disrupt morning people — the art lies in creating flexibility and adjusting accordingly.

drillQuestions.question: An explanation pitched at a graduate seminar will _____ a general audience.
"Reading the room" means sensing the audience's _____.
"The art lies in" means the real _____ is found in.
"Pitched at" means _____ to a specific level.

sentences.notes: 양극단의 실패(bore an expert / alienate a general audience)를 대칭적으로 제시한 뒤, 대시 뒤에 "the art lies in reading the room"이라는 실천적 결론을 넣는다. "pitched at"이라는 비유적 동사가 설명의 수준 조절을 물리적 행위처럼 표현한다.

koreanAlignment.en: An explanation that works for a ten-year-old
will bore an expert
and an explanation pitched at a graduate seminar
will alienate a general audience
the art lies in reading the room and adjusting accordingly
koreanAlignment.ko: 기술은 분위기를 읽고 그에 따라 조정하는 데 있다

pronunciationNotes: alienate: /ˈeɪliəneɪt/ — 첫 음절에 강세. "에일리어네이트"에 가깝다. alien과 같은 어근.
accordingly: /əˈkɔːrdɪŋli/ — 두 번째 음절에 강세. "어코딩리"에 가깝다.

sentences.original: Analogies are among the most powerful tools for this purpose, because they map unfamiliar concepts onto structures the listener already understands.
sentences.translation: 비유는 이 목적을 위한 가장 강력한 도구 중 하나인데, 낯선 개념을 청자가 이미 이해하고 있는 구조에 대응시키기 때문이다.

grammarElements.text: Analogies
grammarElements.label: 주어 (복수 명사)

grammarElements.text: are
grammarElements.label: 동사 (현재형)

grammarElements.text: among the most powerful tools for this purpose
grammarElements.label: 보어 (전치사구 + 최상급)

grammarElements.text: because they map unfamiliar concepts onto structures the listener already understands
grammarElements.label: because 이유절 (목적격 관계대명사 생략)

vocabulary.example: Good analogies make abstract ideas concrete.
The teacher mapped the new concept onto a familiar example.
Analogies help when the topic is unfamiliar.

expressions.usage: among + 최상급 구조로 높은 위치를 강조하되 유일성은 주장하지 않는 신중한 표현
앞에서 논의한 목표를 간결하게 되받는 연결 표현

patterns.pattern: [Tool/Method] is among the most powerful tools for [purpose], because it maps [unfamiliar] onto [familiar]
patterns.explanation: 특정 도구나 방법의 힘을 최상급으로 제시하고, because절로 그 작동 원리를 "낯선 것을 익숙한 것에 대응시킨다"는 구조로 설명하는 패턴.
examples: Storytelling is among the most powerful tools for persuasion, because it maps abstract principles onto concrete human experiences the listener can feel.
Visualization is among the most powerful tools for data communication, because it maps numerical relationships onto spatial patterns the brain processes intuitively.

drillQuestions.question: Analogies _____ unfamiliar concepts onto familiar structures.
"Among the most powerful" means one of the _____.
Analogies work because the listener already _____ the familiar structure.
"For this purpose" refers back to the purpose of _____ things simply.

sentences.notes: 비교적 짧고 명쾌한 문장으로, 비유의 작동 원리를 "map onto"라는 인지 과학적 동사로 정확하게 포착한다. 목적격 관계대명사가 생략된 구조(structures [that] the listener already understands)에 주의해야 한다.

koreanAlignment.en: Analogies are among the most powerful tools for this purpose
koreanAlignment.ko: 비유는 이 목적을 위한 가장 강력한 도구 중 하나인데

koreanAlignment.en: because they map unfamiliar concepts
onto structures the listener already understands
koreanAlignment.ko: 청자가 이미 이해하고 있는 구조에 대응시키기 때문이다

pronunciationNotes: analogies: /əˈnælədʒiz/ — 두 번째 음절에 강세. "어낼러지즈"에 가깝다. -gies를 /dʒiz/로 발음.
unfamiliar: /ˌʌnfəˈmɪliər/ — 세 번째 음절에 강세. "언퍼밀리어"에 가깝다. un- 접두사가 "~이 아닌"을 뜻함.

sentences.original: Saying "DNA is like a recipe book for building a body" is not scientifically precise, but it gives a non-specialist a functional framework that more precise language would not.
sentences.translation: "DNA는 몸을 만들기 위한 레시피 북과 같다"라고 말하는 것은 과학적으로 정확하지는 않지만, 더 정확한 언어로는 제공하지 못할 기능적 틀을 비전문가에게 제공한다.

grammarElements.text: Saying "DNA is like a recipe book for building a body"
grammarElements.label: 주어 (동명사 + 직접 인용문)

grammarElements.text: is not
grammarElements.label: 동사 + 부정

grammarElements.text: scientifically precise
grammarElements.label: 보어 (부사 + 형용사)

grammarElements.text: but
grammarElements.label: 등위접속사 (양보)

grammarElements.text: it gives a non-specialist a functional framework that more precise language would not
grammarElements.label: 양보절 (이중목적어 + 관계절 + 가정법)

vocabulary.example: Scientific language must be precise.
Non-specialists benefit from clear analogies.
A good analogy provides a functional framework for understanding.

expressions.usage: 정확성의 한계를 인정하면서도 실용적 가치를 옹호하는 양보 표현
완벽하지는 않지만 실제로 쓸모 있는 이해 구조를 가리키는 학술적 표현

patterns.pattern: Saying "[analogy]" is not [perfectly accurate], but it gives [audience] a [useful structure] that [more accurate approach] would not
patterns.explanation: 비유의 불완전성을 먼저 인정하고, but으로 전환하여 실용적 가치가 정확성보다 더 중요한 경우가 있음을 주장하는 양보 패턴. 가정법(would not)이 대안의 한계를 암시한다.
examples: Saying "the economy is like a machine" is not technically precise, but it gives citizens a functional framework that more nuanced models would not.
Saying "your immune system is an army" is not medically exact, but it gives patients a useful mental picture that clinical terminology would not.

drillQuestions.question: Saying "DNA is like a recipe book" is not scientifically _____.
It gives a non-specialist a functional _____.
"More precise language would not" means exact language would _____ to provide that understanding.
A "non-specialist" is someone who is not an _____.

sentences.notes: 비유의 가치를 인정하면서도 한계를 명시하는 균형 잡힌 문장이다. 관계절 끝의 "would not"이 가정법으로 "더 정확한 언어를 썼더라도 이 효과는 얻지 못했을 것"이라는 반사실적 주장을 압축한다. 동명사 주어(Saying "~")가 인용문을 자연스럽게 통합한다.

koreanAlignment.en: Saying "DNA is like a recipe book for building a body"
koreanAlignment.ko: "DNA는 몸을 만들기 위한 레시피 북과 같다"라고 말하는 것은

koreanAlignment.en: is not scientifically precise
but it gives a non-specialist a functional framework
that more precise language would not

pronunciationNotes: precise: /prɪˈsaɪs/ — 두 번째 음절에 강세. "프리사이스"에 가깝다. -cise를 /saɪs/로 발음.
framework: /ˈfreɪmwɜːrk/ — 첫 음절에 강세. "프레임워크"에 가깝다. 복합어로 첫 요소에 강세.

sentences.original: The best explainers also know when to stop — they resist the temptation to add "just one more detail" and instead leave the listener with a clean, memorable takeaway that can serve as a foundation for deeper learning later.
sentences.translation: 최고의 설명자는 또한 언제 멈춰야 하는지를 안다 — 그들은 "딱 한 가지만 더"를 추가하려는 유혹을 물리치고, 대신 청자에게 나중에 더 깊은 학습의 토대가 될 수 있는 깔끔하고 기억에 남는 핵심 요약을 남긴다.

grammarElements.text: The best explainers
grammarElements.label: 주어 (최상급 + 명사)

grammarElements.text: also know
grammarElements.label: 부사 + 동사

grammarElements.text: when to stop
grammarElements.label: 목적어 (의문사 + to 부정사)

grammarElements.text: they resist the temptation to add "just one more detail"
grammarElements.label: 대시 뒤 설명절 1 (to 부정사 수식)

grammarElements.text: and instead leave the listener with a clean, memorable takeaway that can serve as a foundation for deeper learning later
grammarElements.label: 병렬 설명절 2 (관계절 포함)

vocabulary.example: Good speakers resist the urge to overexplain.
The temptation to add more detail is strong but counterproductive.
A good presentation ends with a clear takeaway.
Basic understanding serves as a foundation for advanced study.

expressions.usage: 적절한 시점에서 그만두는 판단력을 가리키는 표현
과도한 설명으로 이끄는 심리적 함정을 인용 부호로 표시한 관용적 표현

patterns.pattern: The best [practitioners] also know when to stop — they resist the temptation to [overdo] and instead [leave the audience with a positive outcome]
patterns.explanation: 최고의 실천가가 가진 "절제의 기술"을 대시 뒤에 resist와 instead의 대비로 구체화하는 패턴. 과잉과 절제의 대조가 핵심이다.
examples: The best writers also know when to stop — they resist the temptation to add "just one more paragraph" and instead leave the reader with a sharp closing line that lingers.
The best cooks also know when to stop — they resist the temptation to add "just one more seasoning" and instead leave the dish with a clean, balanced flavor.

drillQuestions.question: The best explainers know when to _____.
They resist the _____ to add more detail.
A "takeaway" is the main _____ the listener remembers.
The takeaway can serve as a _____ for deeper learning later.

sentences.notes: 설명의 절제를 논하는 문장. "when to stop"이라는 간결한 목적어가 핵심 기술을 압축하고, 대시 뒤에 resist와 instead의 대비가 과잉 설명의 유혹과 절제의 미덕을 대조한다. "just one more detail"을 인용 부호로 감싸 과도한 설명의 심리적 함정을 생생하게 포착한다.

koreanAlignment.en: The best explainers also know when to stop
koreanAlignment.ko: 최고의 설명자는 또한 언제 멈춰야 하는지를 안다

koreanAlignment.en: they resist the temptation to add "just one more detail"
koreanAlignment.ko: "딱 한 가지만 더"를 추가하려는 유혹을 물리치고

koreanAlignment.en: and instead leave the listener with a clean, memorable takeaway
koreanAlignment.ko: 대신 청자에게 깔끔하고 기억에 남는 핵심 요약을 남긴다

koreanAlignment.en: that can serve as a foundation for deeper learning later
koreanAlignment.ko: 나중에 더 깊은 학습의 토대가 될 수 있는

pronunciationNotes: temptation: /tempˈteɪʃən/ — 두 번째 음절에 강세. "템테이션"에 가깝다. p 다음의 t를 두 번 발음하지 않는다.
memorable: /ˈmemərəbəl/ — 첫 음절에 강세. "메머러블"에 가깝다. -able을 /əbəl/로 약하게 발음.

sentences.original: Overexplaining is a surprisingly common failure mode, driven by the explainer's anxiety that the audience might miss something, but the effect is usually the opposite: too much information overwhelms the listener and obscures the very point it was meant to clarify.
sentences.translation: 과도한 설명은 청중이 뭔가를 놓칠 수 있다는 설명자의 불안에 의해 추동되는 놀라울 정도로 흔한 실패 양식인데, 효과는 보통 정반대이다: 너무 많은 정보가 청자를 압도하고 명확히 하려던 바로 그 요점을 흐리게 한다.

grammarElements.text: Overexplaining
grammarElements.label: 주어 (동명사)

grammarElements.text: is
grammarElements.label: 동사 (현재형)

grammarElements.text: a surprisingly common failure mode
grammarElements.label: 보어 (부사 + 형용사 + 명사구)

grammarElements.text: driven by the explainer's anxiety that the audience might miss something
grammarElements.label: 과거분사구 (that 동격절 포함)

grammarElements.text: but the effect is usually the opposite: too much information overwhelms the listener and obscures the very point it was meant to clarify
grammarElements.label: but 반전절 (콜론 뒤 설명 포함)

vocabulary.example: Overexplaining can confuse rather than clarify.
Overconfidence is a common failure mode in decision-making.
Too much detail overwhelms the reader.
Excessive detail obscures the main point.

expressions.usage: 공학/시스템 분석에서 차용한 표현으로, 반복되는 실패 유형을 가리킨다

expressions.phrase: the very point it was meant to clarify
expressions.usage: very가 강조 형용사로 "바로 그"를 뜻하며, 역설적 결과를 부각한다

patterns.pattern: [Overaction] is a surprisingly common failure mode, driven by [anxiety], but the effect is usually the opposite: [excessive input] overwhelms [audience] and obscures [the intended goal]
patterns.explanation: 과잉 행동이 불안에서 비롯되지만 의도와 반대 결과를 낳는다는 역설을 콜론 뒤에 구체화하는 패턴. "driven by"가 원인, "but the effect is the opposite"가 반전을 담당한다.
examples: Overprotecting children is a surprisingly common failure mode, driven by parents' anxiety that something might go wrong, but the effect is usually the opposite: too much shielding weakens resilience and removes the very experiences it was meant to guard against.

Overplanning is a surprisingly common failure mode, driven by the manager's anxiety that the team might drift, but the effect is usually the opposite: too many constraints stifle creativity and prevent the very innovation it was meant to enable.

drillQuestions.question: Overexplaining is driven by the explainer's _____.
The effect of overexplaining is usually the _____.
Too much information _____ the listener.
"Failure mode" is a term borrowed from _____ analysis.

sentences.notes: "failure mode"라는 공학 용어의 차용이 글의 분석적 성격을 강화한다. "driven by ~ anxiety"의 과거분사구가 원인을 간결하게 제시하고, 콜론 뒤의 "too much information overwhelms ~ and obscures ~" 병렬이 과잉 설명의 역설적 결과를 명쾌하게 드러낸다.

koreanAlignment.en: Overexplaining is a surprisingly common failure mode
koreanAlignment.ko: 과도한 설명은 놀라울 정도로 흔한 실패 양식인데

koreanAlignment.en: driven by the explainer's anxiety that the audience might miss something
koreanAlignment.ko: 청중이 뭔가를 놓칠 수 있다는 설명자의 불안에 의해 추동되는

koreanAlignment.en: but the effect is usually the opposite
too much information overwhelms the listener
and obscures the very point it was meant to clarify
koreanAlignment.ko: 명확히 하려던 바로 그 요점을 흐리게 한다

pronunciationNotes: overwhelms: /ˌoʊvərˈwelmz/ — 세 번째 음절에 강세. "오버웰므즈"에 가깝다. wh를 /w/로 발음.
anxiety: /æŋˈzaɪəti/ — 두 번째 음절에 강세. "앵자이어티"에 가깝다. x를 /ɡz/로 발음.

sentences.original: In a world that grows more specialized every year, the people who can translate between domains — who can take what the scientist discovered, what the engineer built, or what the policy analyst recommended and make it legible to a broader public — perform a service that is as valuable as the original work itself.
sentences.translation: 매년 더 전문화되는 세계에서, 영역 간 번역을 할 수 있는 사람들 — 과학자가 발견한 것, 엔지니어가 만든 것, 또는 정책 분석가가 권고한 것을 가져다가 더 넓은 대중이 읽을 수 있게 만드는 사람들 — 은 원래 작업 자체만큼이나 가치 있는 서비스를 수행한다.

grammarElements.text: In a world that grows more specialized every year
grammarElements.label: 전치사구 (관계절 수식)

grammarElements.text: the people who can translate between domains
grammarElements.label: 주어 (관계절 수식)

grammarElements.text: who can take what the scientist discovered, what the engineer built, or what the policy analyst recommended and make it legible to a broader public
grammarElements.label: 대시 삽입 관계절 (간접의문문 3개 병렬)

grammarElements.text: perform
grammarElements.label: 동사 (현재형)

grammarElements.text: a service that is as valuable as the original work itself
grammarElements.label: 목적어 (관계절 + 동등비교)

vocabulary.example: The world grows more specialized with each passing year.
The ability to translate between domains is rare and valuable.

vocabulary.meaning: 읽을 수 있는, 이해할 수 있는
vocabulary.example: Good science writing makes research legible to the public.
The policy analyst recommended new regulations.

expressions.usage: 한 전문 분야의 지식을 다른 분야나 대중이 이해할 수 있는 형태로 바꾸는 것을 비유적으로 표현

expressions.phrase: as valuable as the original work itself
expressions.usage: as A as B 동등비교로 번역/설명 작업의 가치를 원래 연구와 동등하게 격상시키는 표현

patterns.pattern: In a world that [trend], the people who can [bridging skill] — who can [specific examples] and make it [accessible] — perform a service that is as valuable as [the original]
patterns.explanation: 시대적 맥락을 먼저 설정하고, 대시 삽입구로 구체적 역할을 예시한 뒤, 그 가치를 동등비교(as ~ as)로 원래 작업과 동급으로 격상시키는 패턴. 이 글에서 가장 긴 문장이다.
examples: In a world that produces more data every day, the people who can interpret it for decision-makers — who can take what the algorithm flagged, what the survey revealed, and what the trend suggests and present it clearly — perform a service that is as essential as the research itself.

In a culture that grows more fragmented every year, the people who can connect across divides — who can take what one group values, what another fears, and what both need and articulate it fairly — perform a service that is as important as the policies themselves.

drillQuestions.question: The people who can _____ between domains perform a valuable service.
"Legible to a broader public" means _____ by ordinary people.
This service is described as being _____ valuable as the original work.
"In a world that grows more specialized" sets a _____ context.

sentences.notes: 이 글에서 가장 긴 문장으로, 전치사구(In a world that ~), 주어 관계절(the people who can ~), 대시 삽입 관계절(who can take what ~ what ~ or what ~ and make ~), 목적어 관계절(a service that is ~)이 모두 결합된 다층 구조이다. 세 개의 간접의문문(what the scientist ~ / what the engineer ~ / what the policy analyst ~)이 병렬로 나열되어 "영역 간 번역"의 범위를 확장한다.

koreanAlignment.en: In a world that grows more specialized every year
the people who can translate between domains
koreanAlignment.ko: 영역 간 번역을 할 수 있는 사람들은

koreanAlignment.en: who can take what the scientist discovered, what the engineer built, or what the policy analyst recommended
koreanAlignment.ko: 과학자가 발견한 것, 엔지니어가 만든 것, 또는 정책 분석가가 권고한 것을 가져다가

koreanAlignment.en: and make it legible to a broader public
koreanAlignment.ko: 더 넓은 대중이 읽을 수 있게 만드는 사람들

koreanAlignment.en: perform a service that is as valuable as the original work itself
koreanAlignment.ko: 원래 작업 자체만큼이나 가치 있는 서비스를 수행한다

pronunciationNotes: legible: /ˈledʒəbəl/ — 첫 음절에 강세. "레저블"에 가깝다. g를 /dʒ/로 발음. illegible(읽을 수 없는)과 대비.
specialized: /ˈspeʃəlaɪzd/ — 첫 음절에 강세. "스페셜라이즈드"에 가깝다. -ized를 /aɪzd/로 발음.

sentences.original: Explaining hard things simply is not a lesser form of intelligence; it is intelligence in its most generous and socially useful form.
sentences.translation: 어려운 것을 쉽게 설명하는 것은 열등한 형태의 지성이 아니다; 그것은 가장 관대하고 사회적으로 유용한 형태의 지성이다.

grammarElements.text: Explaining hard things simply
grammarElements.label: 주어 (동명사구)

grammarElements.text: is not
grammarElements.label: 동사 + 부정

grammarElements.text: a lesser form of intelligence
grammarElements.label: 보어 (비교급 형용사 + 명사구)

grammarElements.text: it is intelligence in its most generous and socially useful form
grammarElements.label: 세미콜론 뒤 재정의절 (최상급)

vocabulary.example: Simplification is often dismissed as a lesser skill.
Clear communication is a form of intelligence.
Sharing knowledge generously is a sign of true understanding.

expressions.phrase: is not a lesser form of
expressions.usage: 어떤 것이 과소평가되어서는 안 된다는 주장을 할 때 쓰는 표현

expressions.phrase: in its most generous and socially useful form
expressions.usage: 최상급으로 설명 능력의 가치를 최고 수준으로 격상시키는 표현

patterns.pattern: [Gerund] is not a lesser form of [quality]; it is [quality] in its most [positive adjective] form
patterns.explanation: 세미콜론으로 연결된 부정-긍정 재정의 구조. 먼저 열등하다는 인식을 부정하고, 뒤에서 오히려 최고의 형태라고 격상시키는 강력한 수사적 패턴.
examples: Teaching is not a lesser form of scholarship; it is scholarship in its most impactful and enduring form.
Listening is not a lesser form of conversation; it is conversation in its most respectful and productive form.

drillQuestions.question: Explaining hard things simply is not a _____ form of intelligence.
It is intelligence in its most _____ and socially useful form.
The semicolon connects a _____ claim with a positive redefinition.
"A lesser form" implies something that is considered _____ valuable.

sentences.notes: 에세이의 핵심 주장을 압축한 선언적 문장. "is not a lesser form of intelligence"의 부정과 "it is intelligence in its most generous ~ form"의 최상급 긍정이 세미콜론으로 극적으로 대비된다. 짧고 리듬감 있는 구조가 기억에 남는 슬로건 효과를 낸다.

koreanAlignment.en: Explaining hard things simply
is not a lesser form of intelligence
it is intelligence in its most generous and socially useful form
koreanAlignment.ko: 그것은 가장 관대하고 사회적으로 유용한 형태의 지성이다

pronunciationNotes: intelligence: /ɪnˈtelɪdʒəns/ — 두 번째 음절에 강세. "인텔리전스"에 가깝다. -gence를 /dʒəns/로 발음.
generous: /ˈdʒenərəs/ — 첫 음절에 강세. "제너러스"에 가깝다. g를 /dʒ/로 발음.

sentences.original: If you can make someone else understand what you understand, you have done more than demonstrate knowledge — you have created it in another mind, and that is the deepest purpose of communication.
sentences.translation: 당신이 이해하는 것을 다른 사람이 이해하게 만들 수 있다면, 당신은 지식을 보여 주는 것 이상을 한 것이다 — 당신은 그것을 다른 마음 안에 창조한 것이며, 그것이 소통의 가장 깊은 목적이다.

grammarElements.text: If you can make someone else understand what you understand
grammarElements.label: 조건절 (사역동사 make + 간접의문문)

grammarElements.text: you
grammarElements.label: 주어

grammarElements.text: have done
grammarElements.label: 동사 (현재완료)

grammarElements.text: more than demonstrate knowledge
grammarElements.label: 목적어 (비교)

grammarElements.text: you have created it in another mind, and that is the deepest purpose of communication
grammarElements.label: 대시 뒤 설명절 (병렬 + 지시대명사 결론)

vocabulary.example: Explaining a concept demonstrates true understanding.
Teaching creates knowledge in another mind.
The deepest purpose of communication is shared understanding.
Communication is more than exchanging words.

expressions.usage: 행위의 가치가 단순한 시연을 넘어선다는 것을 강조하는 비교 표현
최상급(deepest)으로 소통의 궁극적 존재 이유를 규정하는 격식적 표현

patterns.pattern: If you can [make someone understand], you have done more than [surface achievement] — you have [deeper achievement], and that is [ultimate purpose]
patterns.explanation: 조건절로 능력을 제시하고, "more than A — you have B" 구조로 표면적 성취를 넘어선 더 깊은 의미를 부여한 뒤, "and that is the deepest purpose of ~"로 궁극적 가치를 선언하는 결론 패턴.
examples: If you can make a stranger feel at home, you have done more than show hospitality — you have created belonging, and that is the deepest purpose of community.
If you can make a student want to learn more, you have done more than deliver a lesson — you have ignited curiosity, and that is the deepest purpose of education.

drillQuestions.question: You have done more than _____ knowledge.
You have _____ knowledge in another mind.
"That is the deepest purpose of communication" uses "that" to refer to _____.
"If you can make someone else understand" uses "make" as a _____ verb.

sentences.notes: 글의 결론으로, 설명을 "지식의 전달"에서 "지식의 창조"로 격상시키는 핵심적 재정의를 담는다. 사역동사 make(make someone else understand)가 설명의 능동적 성격을 강조하고, "created it in another mind"라는 은유가 소통의 생성적(generative) 본질을 포착한다. "the deepest purpose of communication"이라는 최상급 선언으로 에세이 전체를 마무리한다.

koreanAlignment.en: If you can make someone else understand what you understand
koreanAlignment.ko: 당신이 이해하는 것을 다른 사람이 이해하게 만들 수 있다면

koreanAlignment.en: you have done more than demonstrate knowledge
koreanAlignment.ko: 당신은 지식을 보여 주는 것 이상을 한 것이다

koreanAlignment.en: you have created it in another mind
koreanAlignment.ko: 당신은 그것을 다른 마음 안에 창조한 것이며

koreanAlignment.en: and that is the deepest purpose of communication

pronunciationNotes: demonstrate: /ˈdemənstreɪt/ — 첫 음절에 강세. "데먼스트레이트"에 가깝다. -strate를 /streɪt/로 발음.
communication: /kəˌmjuːnɪˈkeɪʃən/ — 네 번째 음절에 강세. "커뮤니케이션"에 가깝다.
